subject: [PATCH v2 1/7] mm: list_lru: lock_list_lru_of_memcg() cannot return NULL if !skip_empty

2026-03-12.patch_summary: This patch series removes unnecessary NULL checks in the list_lru code, which is responsible for managing LRU (Least Recently Used) lists of memory allocations. The changes are based on the observation that acquiring a lock to access these lists will never fail.
